title: Author PROP for source-file rescue gap — decider's coherent multi-file changes lose source files when push 403s
summary: PROP-014 rollout exposed an adjacent bug class. Decider's self-apply procedure can produce coherent multi-file changes (e.g., add a new schema value to data/predictions.json AND add the corresponding entry to test.js validAuthorStatuses array). When decider's push 403s, universal-pusher rescue copies committed-but-unpushed files to FUSE — but test.js (and other source code in NEVER_PUSH) is intentionally not rescuable because workspace-sync's NEVER_PUSH list filters it out. Net: data file changes survive (rescued via FUSE → workspace-sync → git), source-code changes are silently dropped. Result: data and source drift apart in origin/main, tests fail. Observed today: PRED-105 'tacit_withdrawal' / 'dome_silent_terminal' values landed in data/predictions.json without the test.js valid-sets update — caused 2 test failures in main until operator manual fix. Tinker authors a PROP for the structural fix. PROPOSE ONLY.

context.trigger: Operator-flagged 2026-05-02 ~15:30Z after observing 2 test failures in main caused by decider's coherent-multi-file change losing the test.js half during 403 → universal-pusher rescue. Operator manual-fixed at commit b2bfd74 (added the new values to test.js valid-sets). The operator manual fix doesn't address the structural bug — next time decider modifies test.js + data/* coherently and gets 403'd, the same drift happens.
context.bug_mechanism: 1. Decider's self-apply procedure (per monitor/prompts/reference/decider-patches-and-selfapply.md) clones fresh, applies patches via apply-patches.js, runs tests, commits + pushes. 2. If patches touch BOTH a data file (data/predictions.json) AND a source file (test.js, build-scripts/*, build.js, CLAUDE.md, generate-html.js), all changes are in the same commit. 3. If push 403s: universal-pusher rescue block copies a fixed list of data files to FUSE (data/wins.json, data/sections.json, data/predictions.json, data/uncounted-failures.json, monitor/decisions/open-issues.json, monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json, monitor/curmudgeon/priority-queue.json, monitor/decisions/applied-patches/*.json — see decider-patches-and-selfapply.md universal_pusher_rescue block). Source files are NOT in this list because (a) NEVER_PUSH semantics: workspace-sync doesn't push source files from FUSE → git on principle, source code flows git → FUSE one-way. 4. Workspace-sync rescue picks up the data files, pushes to origin/main. The source file changes are lost when decider's clone is destroyed. 5. origin/main now has the data delta but not the source delta. Tests fail; build may fail; schema mismatches accumulate.

observed_instance.decider_runs: 09:57Z (commit 3f34cec, introduced 'tacit_withdrawal' our_verdict for PRED-105) and 13:19Z (introduced 'dome_silent_terminal' author_status; push 403d, rescued via workspace-sync 14:05Z commit ed52186). The 13:19Z run probably had a test.js update in clone but lost it at rescue time.
observed_instance.symptom: 2 test failures in main: 'PRED-105 author_status dome_silent_terminal is valid' FAIL, 'PRED-105 our_verdict tacit_withdrawal is valid' FAIL. Tests went 3518/0 → 3516/2.
observed_instance.operator_fix: commit b2bfd74 added both values to validAuthorStatuses + validOurVerdicts in test.js. Tests back to 3518/0. Manual fix; structural bug remains.
observed_instance.visibility: Caught by operator running test.js manually during PROP-014 rollout work. Otherwise would have been caught by integrity agent's daily 9am run — but that's a 9-hour TTL on green tests.

context.related_to_prop_014: Same family as PROP-014 phantom-integration in spirit (agent's local state diverges from origin/main due to push failure), but different mechanism. PROP-014 Mech 1a addresses data/ledger files where decider lies about completion. THIS bug is about coherent multi-file commits where the source-code half can't survive rescue. PROP-014's verifier won't catch it because the verification_pattern only checks data files.

structure.forensic_trace: Scan the last 14 days of decider runs + push-failure files. Identify any decider commit that touched BOTH a data file (data/*.json) AND a source file (anything in NEVER_PUSH per CLAUDE.md: docs/, build-scripts/, build.js, test.js, CLAUDE.md, monitor/prompts/*.md). For each such commit: did the push succeed (single commit visible in main with all files), or fail (rescue commit shows only data file half)? The instance count is the calibration data. Today's PRED-105 case is one known instance; there may be others undetected.
structure.taxonomy: Is this one bug, or several? E.g., source code that's needed downstream (test.js validates data; if data has new value but test doesn't, tests fail) is a tighter coupling than source code that's just operational (a comment update in build.js). Tinker should classify which file types in NEVER_PUSH have actual coupling to data files vs which are independent. test.js is the worst-case (immediate test failure). build-scripts/generate-html.js could mismatch — patches the prose templates; if data has new field but generator doesn't render it, missing UI but tests pass. CLAUDE.md is documentation-only — drift is silent.
structure.proposed_fix_options:
Tinker should propose at least 3 mechanisms with trade-offs:
  (A) HALT-AND-REQUIRE-HUMAN. If decider's apply-patches.js output shows any patch targeting NEVER_PUSH files, halt before commit. Write to monitor/decisions/suggested-patches-NNN.json with `requires_human_to_apply: true`. Operator commits the source-file half manually.
  (B) DETECT-AND-WARN PRE-FLIGHT. Same as (A) but softer: decider applies the patch but writes a HARD WARNING in the daily report flagging the multi-file commit and tells operator to verify both halves landed. Less coercive, more brittle.
  (C) UNIVERSAL-PUSHER EXPANSION. Allow specific source files (test.js maybe) into the universal-pusher rescue list. Most direct fix; trade-off: violates NEVER_PUSH semantics (workspace-sync would have to push source code that round-tripped through FUSE — exactly what NEVER_PUSH was designed to prevent). Operator's lean: probably wrong tradeoff.
  (D) SCHEMA-OUT-OF-SOURCE. Move schema validation values (validAuthorStatuses, validOurVerdicts arrays) out of test.js into a data file like data/schema-values.json. Then test.js reads from data/. Decider modifying schema becomes a data-file change which IS rescuable. This is a more invasive refactor but eliminates the test.js coupling entirely. Could combine with (A) for other source-code coupling.
  Tinker may propose other mechanisms not on this list. Argue for one as the recommended primary fix.
structure.measurement_plan: How would we know the fix worked? E.g., 'zero source-file drift incidents in main over 14d post-rollout' for option (A). Define metrics + disprove conditions per option.
structure.sequencing: If multiple mechanisms ship together, what order? Can option (D) ship as a refactor independent of (A)? Are there interactions?

deliverable_requested.do_not_implement: DO NOT make any prompt edits, schema refactors, or file mutations outside the PROP file itself + lifecycle fields on this directive. Operator reviews and authorizes implementation in a separate step.

non_goals: Do NOT propose making decider's direct push more reliable — that's environmental, out of scope (per HNOTE-OPERATOR-PAT-DIAGNOSIS-CORRECTION-001).
Do NOT touch PROP-014 or its mechanisms — this is a sibling bug, not a PROP-014 amendment.
Do NOT widen the universal-pusher rescue list without explicit trade-off analysis showing the NEVER_PUSH violation is justified for that file type.
Do NOT broaden scope to other multi-file-coherence issues that aren't about NEVER_PUSH (e.g., data-only inter-file coherence is already handled).
Do NOT propose a fix that requires changes outside the dome-model-review repo (tooling, infra, etc.).

proposed_fix_spec.note_to_tinker: Operator's lean is option (A) HALT-AND-REQUIRE-HUMAN as the primary fix because it's the simplest, most defensive, and aligns with the existing 'leave for human' patterns elsewhere (e.g., verdict-change patches already do this). Option (D) SCHEMA-OUT-OF-SOURCE is appealing for the test.js case specifically but may be over-investment for one schema. Tinker may disagree — challenge freely. The directive's authority is the bug + the request for a PROP, not the specific framework operator gestured at.

completion_criteria.forensic_trace_quality: 14-day scan of decider commits enumerated; per-instance whether data and source halves both landed; instance count + distribution.
completion_criteria.options_argued: ≥3 mechanisms proposed with trade-offs. Recommended primary cited with reasoning.
completion_criteria.measurement_pre_committed: Metrics defined before implementation. Disprove conditions explicit.
completion_criteria.sequencing_argued: Order of execution if multiple mechanisms; independence between mechanisms noted.
completion_criteria.mark_directive_completed: Set status:'completed' with completed_at + completed_by_run + prop_id_authored fields appended (per DIRECTIVE-LIFECYCLE additive-edit exception in CLAUDE.md). Do not mutate other fields.

cross_references: PROP-014 forensic_trace.NC-1 (narrative confabulation — somewhat related to agent local state diverging from origin/main)
CLAUDE.md File Ownership Rules NEVER_PUSH list
Today's commit b2bfd74 (operator manual fix for the observed PRED-105 schema drift)
HNOTE-OPERATOR-PAT-DIAGNOSIS-CORRECTION-001 (push 403 is environmental — not the variable to fix)
